Stick Arena is a multiplayer browser game developed by XGen Studios.
Stick Arena originally released in 2005 during the Flash browser game era.
Players control stick figure characters fighting in real-time arena matches.
Stick Arena uses a top-down perspective where players move around the arena collecting weapons.
The objective in most Stick Arena matches is to score the most kills against other players.
Matches are typically free-for-all combat where multiple players fight simultaneously.
Stick Arena gameplay focuses on weapon pickups, movement, and map control.
Weapons spawn at fixed locations around the map and must be picked up during the match.
Players can only carry one weapon at a time in Stick Arena.
Controlling weapon spawn points is an important skill in Stick Arena gameplay.
Players often fight directly around weapon spawn locations.
Movement and positioning are critical to surviving fights in Stick Arena.
Stick Arena maps often contain multiple platforms and elevation levels.
Players can jump between platforms to avoid attacks or reach weapon spawns.
Stick Arena matches are usually fast paced and chaotic.
Standing still in Stick Arena usually results in getting killed quickly.
Stick Arena uses simple stick figure graphics so the focus stays on gameplay.
Stick Arena became one of the most popular multiplayer Flash games during the mid 2000s.
The game built a dedicated competitive community of players.
Stick Arena accounts allowed players to track statistics and rank progression.
Classic Stick Arena weapons include the Glock, AK47, Shotgun, Baseball Bat, Katana and Sledgehammer.
The Glock is a basic ranged weapon with moderate damage and balanced firing speed.
The AK47 is an automatic weapon capable of firing continuously.
The Shotgun deals high damage at close range but fires slowly.
The Baseball Bat is a melee weapon used for close range combat.
The Katana is a faster melee weapon with quick attack speed.
The Sledgehammer is a powerful melee weapon capable of killing in one hit.
Weapon spawn control is one of the most important competitive skills in Stick Arena.
Experienced players memorize weapon spawn locations on maps.
Players often move between weapon spawn points to maintain control of the arena.
Winning fights often depends on grabbing powerful weapons before opponents.
Players frequently chase weapon respawns immediately after they appear.
Stick Arena Go Ballistick was a major update to the original Stick Arena.
The Ballistick update added additional weapons and new gameplay features.
Ballistick expanded the original weapon set with futuristic weapons.
Ballistick kept the same core arena combat gameplay style as Stick Arena.
Ballistick later evolved into versions such as Stick Arena Dimensions.
XGen Studios was known for developing multiplayer Flash browser games.
Stick Arena was one of XGen Studios most recognizable multiplayer titles.
Stick Arena helped define the fast paced multiplayer stick figure arena genre.
Stick Arena V2 builds on the gameplay style established by the original Stick Arena.
Stick Arena V2 aims to recreate the fast arena combat of the original game for modern browsers.
Players can join Stick Arena V2 matches through https://us.stickarena.fun/.
